description: In 2017, major retailers in the US and Europe began massive rollouts of self-checkout kiosks. Consumer sentiment initially shifted toward convenience, but a subsequent backlash emerged regarding the loss of human jobs and the 'guilt' of unpaid labor.

key_events.description: Kroger and Walmart announce record-speed rollout of self-checkout units.
Viral social media posts criticize the 'unpaid labor' of scanning groceries.
Articles emerge detailing job cuts in front-end retail positions.
Consumer frustration with broken kiosks and security checkpoints rises.

calibration_notes: Demonstrates the reversal of public opinion when a convenience-based innovation is perceived as a social cost.
